title: App crashes on manual Save with large CSV-imported task lists — encoding error
problem: When a user manually clicks the 'Save' button in the toolbar after editing a large task list (~200 tasks) that was imported from a CSV file, the application crashes. A brief error dialog referencing 'encoding' flashes before the app dies. Auto-save continues to work correctly, and manual save works fine for smaller lists.
root_cause_hypothesis: The manual save code path likely uses a different serialization or encoding routine than auto-save. When processing large lists containing characters introduced by CSV import (e.g., non-UTF-8 characters, special delimiters, or BOM markers), this routine either throws an unhandled encoding exception or triggers a buffer/memory issue at scale. The size threshold suggests either a memory-related failure compounded by encoding conversion overhead, or a character that only appears in the larger imported dataset.
reproduction_steps: Create or obtain a CSV file containing ~200 tasks (include varied characters — accented letters, special symbols, non-ASCII content)
Import the CSV file into TaskFlow
Open the imported task list for editing
Click the 'Save' button in the toolbar
Observe the app crash with a brief encoding-related error dialog
environment: Not specified — likely reproducible across environments given it is tied to a specific code path and data condition
impact: Users who import tasks from CSV files and manually save are at risk of data loss. The workaround (relying on auto-save) is fragile and undiscoverable. Any user working with large imported datasets is affected.
recommended_fix: 1. Compare the manual save handler's serialization/encoding logic against the auto-save path to identify where they diverge. 2. Check for unhandled encoding exceptions in the manual save routine — add proper error handling and user-facing error reporting instead of a crash. 3. Inspect CSV import for character encoding normalization (e.g., ensure UTF-8 conversion on ingest, strip BOM markers). 4. Test the manual save path with large payloads containing non-ASCII characters from CSV sources.
proposed_test_case: Import a CSV file with 200+ tasks containing non-ASCII characters (accented characters, emoji, special symbols, and mixed encodings). Edit the list, then trigger manual save via the toolbar button. Verify the save completes without crashing, the data round-trips correctly, and any encoding issues surface as a recoverable error message rather than a crash.
information_gaps: Exact text of the encoding error dialog
Character content of the original CSV file (encoding, special characters present)
Whether the issue reproduces with a freshly created 200-task list (not imported from CSV)
